template_metadata.purpose: Complete remediation plan for Gov_Reg Runtime Authority Verification and File Identity Remediation — Track B (authority) and Track A (naming)

critical_constraint.name: NO_DISCOVERY_AS_CLOSURE
rules: Do not treat plausible discovery as closure
Do not call a file canonical unless execution and authority checks support it
Do not merge competing architectures into one fake solution
Do not infer that missing support files are harmless
Do not mark single-writer semantics proven unless they are actually proven
Do not call the runtime canonical if orchestrator competition remains unresolved
Every fix must have: executable command, evidence artifact path, pass/fail condition
Items that cannot be automated MUST be marked NOT_AUTOMATABLE
EVERY STEP must have explicit inputs/outputs/invariants defined
All mutations MUST create backups before execution
All failures MUST fail-closed with explicit evidence

task_analysis.original_request: Verify runtime canonical authorities, repair 5 confirmed blockers, resolve 7 open gaps (G1-G7), and remediate 163 Python files across 6 naming-scheme categories

quantified_targets: Resolve 7 open gaps (G1-G7, all currently not closed)
Repair 5 confirmed blockers (WRITE_POLICY mismatch, healthcheck import, ID_SCRIPT_INVENTORY missing, registry_transition __init__ missing imports, WRITE_POLICY filename mismatch)
Reduce active mutation paths from multiple to 1 proven authority

parallel_groups.reason: NOT_AUTOMATABLE design decisions; runs after PH-B1 investigation data is available
Both depend only on PH-B0 completion
All depend on PH-B2 and PH-A1; no mutual conflicts
Depend on PG-PARALLEL-2 outputs; no mutual file conflicts
Strict sequential dependency chain within Track A
Depends on all PG-PARALLEL-3 and PG-A-SEQ phases completing

gates: CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_MAP.json covers all 18 roles with no unresolved contested entries
All 5 confirmed blockers repaired with evidence
All 7 gaps (G1-G7) re-assessed with new status values from hardened model
0 active ID collisions (DOC-SCRIPT or P_) remain
0 byte-identical hash forks remain in working tree
3 diverged forks merged into canonicals with syntax validation
4 dual-ID filenames resolved to single canonical ID each
EXECUTION_VERIFICATION_REPORT.json shows imports_resolve=yes for all canonical authorities
AUTHORITY_ENFORCEMENT_TEST_PLAN.json covers all 8 required test cases
AUDIT_V2_RESULT.json uses hardened status vocabulary (no bare 'found' entries)
steps: Each step produces required evidence artifacts
Each step respects file scope boundaries
Each step produces verifiable git diffs

options: simple
moderate
complex
enterprise
decision_log_entry.rationale: 163 files, 17 phases, 2 tracks, NOT_AUTOMATABLE decisions required, authority verification not just static analysis, production governance contracts required

assumptions: Git repository has .git directory at C:/Users/richg/Gov_Reg
C:/Users/richg/ALL_AI/GOVERNANCE/gates/ is readable but outside repo — read-only access only, no mutations
doc_id_registry_cli.py mint command is available for DOC-SCRIPT ID allocation
Backup writes to .state/evidence/authority_remediation/ will not exceed disk quota
Scripts P_01999000042260305010 through P_01999000042260305020 may be missing and are confirmed targets for investigation
in_scope: Re-assessment of 7 open gaps under hardened status model
Registry shape reconciliation (files[] vs records[] vs entries[] vs files[]+edges[]+entities[])
Authority enforcement test plan (8 required test cases)
Production of all 9 required CLI output files
out_of_scope: Rewriting Python scripts in a different language
Migrating to a different pipeline framework
Executing the gate_runner.py or enforcement_bridge.py outside the repo (read-only audit only)
Merging the Gov_Reg repo with ALL_AI/GOVERNANCE
Automated AST-level code splicing for diverged fork merges (patch files are the deliverable)
definition_of_done: All 7 gaps re-assessed with hardened status values — no bare 'found' entries
All 5 blockers repaired with evidence artifacts
3 diverged forks merged with syntax validation passing
18 ungoverned/date-prefixed files onboarded with proper IDs
Orchestrator boundary decision recorded in ORCHESTRATOR_BOUNDARY_REPORT.json
Evidence trail complete for all steps under .state/evidence/authority_remediation/

file_manifest.description: All files touched during authority remediation and naming remediation across Tracks A and B
file_manifest.purpose: Change tracking, conflict detection, review scoping

global_files.reason: Primary registry — read for authority checks; written only through single approved mutation path after PH-B4
Primary runtime authority candidate — read for import resolution and role verification
Evidence base for all remediation steps

files.reason: Root evidence directory for this remediation
Canonical record of all DQ-1 through DQ-7 decisions plus contract freeze
Audit of gate_runner.py and enforcement_bridge.py from external gates dir
Report on missing REGISTRY_AUTOMATION scripts P_01999000042260305010 through 305020
One canonical/provisional/contested authority per role across 18 roles
Import resolution and usage verification for all recommended authorities
Evidence report for all 5 blocker repairs
Blocker 1: WRITE_POLICY_IDPKG.yaml filename mismatch — create alias or rename
Blocker 2: Fix broken import referencing 125108 instead of 125106
Blocker 3: ID_SCRIPT_INVENTORY.jsonl missing — pre-commit canonicality gate depends on it
Blocker 4: registry_transition __init__.py missing imports for identity_resolver and registry_writer
Classification of all same-role competitor files
Reconcile files[] vs records[] vs entries[] vs files[]+edges[]+entities[] shapes
Binding decision: unified vs two-orchestrator model
Assessment of lookup/join resolver, column runtime engine, post-write pipeline
Rollback manifest for Phase A1 collision fixes
Reference scan results for all reassigned IDs
Rollback manifest with base64-encoded content of deleted forks
Rollback manifest with canonical file snapshots before merge
Rollback manifest for dual-ID resolution
Archive directory for superseded date-prefixed versions
Rollback manifest for ungoverned file onboarding
8-case enforcement test plan for authority governance
Final audit result using hardened status vocabulary
Defensible gap closure assessment for G1-G7

checks: All paths are relative to repo root
No duplicate write permissions in same parallel phase group
All created files have parent directories created first
External gates dir (C:/Users/richg/ALL_AI/GOVERNANCE/gates/) is accessed read-only only

phase_overview.purpose: Read external gates dir (read-only) and audit missing REGISTRY_AUTOMATION scripts
Design decisions DQ-1 through DQ-7 plus contract freeze — all NOT_AUTOMATABLE
Produce CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_MAP.json with one authority per 18 roles
Fix DOC-SCRIPT-0989/0992/0993 and P_01999000042260125106 ID collisions
Run import resolution and usage verification on all recommended authorities
Tag all same-role competitor files across 7 duplicate-role groups
Reconcile registry top-level key shape across all readers and writers
Fix the 5 confirmed blockers with evidence for each
Decide unified vs two-orchestrator model — requires NOT_AUTOMATABLE decision step
Assess lookup/join resolver, column runtime engine, post-write pipeline
Merge 3 diverged hash forks into canonical files
Resolve 4 dual-ID composite filenames per DQ-1/DQ-2 decisions
Archive 4 superseded date-prefixed versions and onboard surviving/standalone files
Onboard 10 date-prefixed + 8 ungoverned files per DQ-5/DQ-6 decisions
Authority enforcement test plan, single-writer enforcement decision, gate wiring

parallel_execution_groups.reason: Can start immediately; produces input data for PH-B0 design decisions
NOT_AUTOMATABLE decision steps; must follow PH-B1
Both depend only on PH-B0; no file overlap
All depend on PG-PARALLEL-1 completing; no mutual write conflicts
Depend on PG-PARALLEL-2; PH-B7 and PH-B8 are analysis-only; PH-B4 patches disjoint files from PH-A3
Strict sequential dependency: A5 depends on A4, A6 depends on A5 (shim targets renamed by A5)
Depends on PG-PARALLEL-3 and PG-A-SEQ all completing

execution_strategy.reason: Two independent tracks (A and B) with defined merge points allow parallel execution within groups; steps within a phase are always sequential

failure_types.description: Counter store or registry JSON is locked by another process
unified_id_allocator.py cannot be imported or located
ID collision still exists in registry after fix attempt
allowed_actions: escalate to maintainer with collision details

failure_types.description: Broken import found during execution verification
allowed_actions: search repo for module providing equivalent implementation
check rename history via git log --follow

failure_types.description: Two or more files found for same role after deduplication pass
allowed_actions: escalate to maintainer with conflict details and both file paths

failure_types.description: Syntax validation or import check fails after patch applied
Phase precondition check fails before execution begins

trigger: NOT_AUTOMATABLE step reached without human input
action: emit structured error report with blocker description
stop execution and await maintainer decision

worktree_isolation.reason: Single developer, two-track sequential-within-phase execution, simpler git workflow preferred
worktree_isolation.alternative: Feature branch per track (feature/track-b-authority, feature/track-a-naming) with backup tags before each phase

enum.L1: Static checks (parse JSON, lint Python, grep search)
enum.L2: Unit-level checks (import resolution, syntax validation, registry parse round-trip)
enum.L3: Integration checks (multi-component, merge + syntax + import chain validation)
enum.L4: End-to-end workflow validation (full gap closure re-assessment, all evidence present)

enforcement.rule: Phase CANNOT complete unless minimum ground truth level passed for ALL steps
enforcement.validator_command: python -c "import json, sys; r=json.load(open(sys.argv[1])); sys.exit(0 if r.get('ground_truth_passed') else 1)" .state/evidence/authority_remediation/{phase}/completion_report.json

PH-B1-INVESTIGATION.purpose: Audit external gate files (read-only) and inventory missing REGISTRY_AUTOMATION scripts to produce raw data for PH-B0 design decisions
invariants: No files mutated in C:/Users/richg/ALL_AI/GOVERNANCE/gates/
No files mutated in the repo during this phase
Only .state/evidence/authority_remediation/PH-B1/ directory written

failure_modes.meaning: gate_runner.py or enforcement_bridge.py not readable in external dir
failure_modes.remediation: Record as unreadable in EXTERNAL_FILE_AUDIT.json with path and error; do not abort

failure_modes.meaning: REGISTRY_AUTOMATION/scripts/ directory does not exist
failure_modes.remediation: Abort with exit 1 and escalation report

PH-B0-BOOTSTRAP.purpose: Record binding design decisions DQ-1 through DQ-7 and freeze contract — all steps are NOT_AUTOMATABLE
invariants: No Python source files modified
No registry mutations performed
Only DESIGN_DECISIONS.json created
postconditions: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/ID/DESIGN_DECISIONS.json')); assert all(f'DQ-{i}' in str(d) for i in range(1,8))"

failure_modes.meaning: One or more DQ-1 through DQ-7 decisions not recorded
failure_modes.remediation: Cannot proceed — maintainer must complete all decisions

PH-B2-AUTHORITY-MAP.purpose: Produce CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_MAP.json with one canonical/provisional/contested authority per each of 18 roles based on investigation data and design decisions
invariants: No source files modified
Every role in the 18-role list must have an entry
No role may have two files both marked canonical
postconditions: python -c "import json; m=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/ID/CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_MAP.json')); roles=[e['role'] for e in m['roles']]; assert len(roles)==18"

failure_modes.meaning: A role has no candidate file at all
failure_modes.remediation: Mark role status as missing in map; do not fabricate a candidate

failure_modes.meaning: Two files both claim canonical status for same role
failure_modes.remediation: Trigger AUTHORITY_CONFLICT failure type; escalate to maintainer

PH-B3-EXEC-VERIFY.purpose: Run import resolution and usage verification on all recommended authorities from PH-B2; produce EXECUTION_VERIFICATION_REPORT.json
invariants: No source files modified
Verification is read-only — no registry mutations
Every role entry in CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_MAP must have a corresponding verification record

failure_modes.meaning: Import check fails for a recommended authority
failure_modes.remediation: Trigger IMPORT_BROKEN failure type; record in report as imports_resolve=no; do not abort phase

PH-B4-BLOCKER-REPAIR.purpose: Fix the 5 confirmed blockers with a backup-before-patch discipline and evidence for each fix
invariants: Every patched file must have a backup created before modification
Registry mutations only through atomic write helper
All 5 blockers addressed — no skips without explicit NOT_AUTOMATABLE recording

failure_modes.meaning: Patched file fails syntax validation
failure_modes.remediation: Restore from backup; record failure in BLOCKER_REMEDIATION_REPORT

failure_modes.meaning: No valid replacement module found for broken import
failure_modes.remediation: Trigger IMPORT_BROKEN failure type; escalate

PH-B5-DUPLICATE-ANALYSIS.purpose: Tag all same-role competitor files across watcher, pipeline runner, patch generator/applier, dir_id handler, reconciler, and orchestrator groups
invariants: No files deleted or renamed in this phase
Analysis is read-only

failure_modes.meaning: File system scan for role duplicates cannot complete
failure_modes.remediation: Record partial results; emit error count in report

PH-B6-REGISTRY-SHAPE.purpose: Enumerate top-level keys used by all registry readers and writers; identify incompatible shapes; recommend canonical shape
invariants: No registry file mutated
Analysis is read-only

failure_modes.meaning: REGISTRY_file.json cannot be parsed as JSON
failure_modes.remediation: Abort with exit 1; do not attempt shape analysis on corrupt file

PH-B7-ORCHESTRATOR-BOUNDARY.purpose: Decide unified vs two-orchestrator model; document owned responsibilities, forbidden responsibilities, and handoff artifacts for each orchestrator
invariants: No source files modified
Decision must be binding — no 'deferred' or 'TBD' orchestrator model

failure_modes.meaning: Maintainer has not provided a binding orchestrator model selection
failure_modes.remediation: Cannot proceed; escalate with NOT_AUTOMATABLE flag

PH-B8-MISSING-COMPONENTS.purpose: Assess lookup/join resolver, column runtime engine, and post-write pipeline — determine embedded, partial, or absent status for each
invariants: No source files created or modified
Analysis is read-only
postconditions: python -c "import json; r=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/.state/evidence/authority_remediation/PH-B8/MISSING_COMPONENTS_REPORT.json')); assert all(k in r for k in ['lookup_join_resolver','column_runtime_engine','post_write_pipeline'])"

failure_modes.meaning: Cannot scan all resolver, reconciler, and column tooling files
failure_modes.remediation: Record partial results; do not claim component is absent if scan was incomplete

PH-A1-COLLISIONS.purpose: Fix DOC-SCRIPT-0989/0992/0993 and P_01999000042260125106 ID collisions by allocating fresh IDs for reassigned files and patching the generator collision check
invariants: Rollback manifest written BEFORE any file is renamed or patched
Registry snapshot taken BEFORE any registry mutation
Exactly one file in each collision pair retains the original ID
Reassigned file gets a freshly allocated ID — no hand-coded ID values
postconditions: python -c "import json; r=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/REGISTRY_file.json')); ids=[e.get('file_id','') for e in r.get('files',r) if isinstance(e,dict)]; assert len(ids)==len(set(ids)), 'duplicate IDs remain'"

failure_modes.meaning: Collision still exists in registry after fix attempt
failure_modes.remediation: Trigger COLLISION_UNRESOLVED failure type; restore from registry snapshot; escalate

failure_modes.meaning: ID allocator returns an ID that is already in use
failure_modes.remediation: Trigger ALLOCATOR_UNAVAILABLE failure type; abort phase

PH-A2-IDENTICAL-FORKS.purpose: Delete 4 byte-identical hash fork duplicates after SHA-256 confirmation; store content in rollback manifest before deletion
invariants: SHA-256 of both files in each pair must match before deletion
Full file content stored base64-encoded in rollback manifest before deletion
If any pair has diverged, redirect to PH-A3 target list and skip deletion for that pair

failure_modes.meaning: Files that should be byte-identical have diverged
failure_modes.remediation: Do not delete; move pair to PH-A3 diverged-forks target list; log in rollback manifest

PH-A3-DIVERGED-FORKS.purpose: Merge unique functionality from 3 diverged hash forks into canonical files using patch files as deliverable; validate syntax after each merge
invariants: Canonical file snapshot saved before any merge is applied
Automated AST-level splicing is prohibited — patch files are the deliverable
Syntax validation runs on canonical after every merge
If syntax check fails, canonical is restored from snapshot immediately

failure_modes.meaning: Merge produces syntax error in canonical
failure_modes.remediation: Restore canonical from snapshot; record failure in rollback manifest; escalate

failure_modes.meaning: Expected hash-suffixed fork file does not exist
failure_modes.remediation: Skip that merge target; record in evidence; do not abort entire phase

PH-A4-DUAL-IDS.purpose: Resolve 4 dual-ID composite filenames to single canonical ID per DQ-1 (registry lookup > body metadata > first ID) and create ALIAS entries for dropped IDs per DQ-2
invariants: Rollback manifest written before any rename
Registry lookup performed before choosing surviving ID
Dropped ID gets ALIAS registry entry pointing to surviving ID
Grep-and-replace performed for all references to old dual-ID filename and dropped ID

failure_modes.meaning: Neither ID in a dual-ID filename has a registry entry
failure_modes.remediation: Apply fallback: keep first ID; log warning in rollback manifest

PH-A5-SUPERSEDED.purpose: Archive 4 superseded date-prefixed versions and onboard 6 standalone date-prefixed files with appropriate P_ or DOC- IDs per DQ-5
invariants: Archived files moved with SHA-256 metadata sidecar
Surviving and standalone files classified per DQ-5 Option B (role-based P_ vs DOC-)
No earlier-version file deleted — moved to .state/archive/superseded/ only

failure_modes.meaning: One or both files in a superseded pair do not exist
failure_modes.remediation: Log warning; skip archival for that pair; do not abort phase

PH-A6-ONBOARD.purpose: Onboard 8 ungoverned plain files (6 standalone + 2 shims) per DQ-6 Option B; shims get DEPRECATED status and updated import targets
invariants: file_id_resolver.py processed first — it is identity-chain critical
Shims get DEPRECATED status in registry, not active
Shim import targets updated to point at Phase 5 renamed canonicals
install_automation.py uses existing body file_id 01999000042260125114 if no collision exists
postconditions: python -c "import json; r=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/REGISTRY_file.json')); files=r.get('files',r); assert any('file_id_resolver' in str(f) for f in files), 'file_id_resolver not onboarded'"

failure_modes.meaning: Phase 5 renamed target for a shim does not exist
failure_modes.remediation: Do not update shim import; log warning; mark shim as needs_manual_review in registry

PH-H-HARDENING.purpose: Produce authority enforcement test plan covering all 8 required cases; make single-writer enforcement decision; wire gate dependencies
invariants: Test plan must cover all 8 required enforcement cases
Single-writer decision must be binding (Option A, B, or C from solution doc section 9.2)
Gate wiring decisions based on ORCHESTRATOR_BOUNDARY_REPORT model

failure_modes.meaning: Single-writer enforcement model not selected
failure_modes.remediation: Escalate to maintainer; cannot produce test plan without binding writer model

PH-PKG-PACKAGE.purpose: Generate AUDIT_V2_RESULT.json with hardened status vocabulary and FINAL_GAP_CLOSURE_REPORT.json with defensible assessments for G1-G7
invariants: AUDIT_V2_RESULT.json must use only hardened status values: candidate_found, implementation_verified, dominant_but_contested, closure_not_proven, missing
No bare 'found' entries allowed in AUDIT_V2_RESULT.json
FINAL_GAP_CLOSURE_REPORT.json must re-assess all 7 gaps G1-G7
Gap may only be marked closed if execution_verification fields are all yes for the canonical authority
postconditions: python -c "import json; r=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/ID/AUDIT_V2_RESULT.json')); assert 'found' not in str(r.get('artifact_groups',[])), 'bare found status detected'"
python -c "import json; r=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/ID/FINAL_GAP_CLOSURE_REPORT.json')); gaps=[g['gap_id'] for g in r['gaps']]; assert all(f'G{i}' in gaps for i in range(1,8))"

failure_modes.meaning: AUDIT_V2_RESULT.json contains a bare found status
failure_modes.remediation: Abort; reclassify the offending entry under the hardened vocabulary before packaging

failure_modes.meaning: One or more of G1-G7 absent from FINAL_GAP_CLOSURE_REPORT.json
failure_modes.remediation: Abort; add missing gap entry before packaging

STEP-B1-001-CREATE-EVIDENCE-DIR.purpose: Create the evidence directory tree for the entire authority remediation under .state/evidence/authority_remediation/
invariants: No existing evidence files overwritten
Only directory creation — no file content written
failure_modes: PREFLIGHT_FAIL if permission denied on .state/

STEP-B1-002-AUDIT-EXTERNAL-GATES.purpose: Read gate_runner.py and enforcement_bridge.py from C:/Users/richg/ALL_AI/GOVERNANCE/gates/ — record class names, function signatures, imports, and readable status; no mutations
invariants: No writes to C:/Users/richg/ALL_AI/GOVERNANCE/gates/
If file is unreadable, record as unreadable with error message — do not abort
failure_modes: If gate_runner.py absent: record readable_source=false; continue
If enforcement_bridge.py absent: record readable_source=false; continue

STEP-B1-003-INVENTORY-MISSING-SCRIPTS.purpose: Check for existence of REGISTRY_AUTOMATION scripts P_01999000042260305010 through P_01999000042260305020; record present vs missing for each
invariants: No files created or deleted in REGISTRY_AUTOMATION/scripts/
Report includes both present and missing entries
failure_modes: If scripts dir absent: abort with PREFLIGHT_FAIL

STEP-B1-004-EMIT-INVESTIGATION-SUMMARY.purpose: Combine EXTERNAL_FILE_AUDIT.json and MISSING_SCRIPTS_REPORT.json into a single PH-B1 completion record for PH-B0 input
invariants: completion_report.json must include ground_truth_passed field
No source files modified
failure_modes: If either input missing: abort with PREFLIGHT_FAIL

STEP-B0-001-DECISIONS-DQ1-DQ4.purpose: Record maintainer decisions for DQ-1 (surviving ID in dual-ID composites), DQ-2 (dropped ID handling), DQ-3 (P_ 125106 collision resolution), DQ-4 (DOC-SCRIPT collision keep rules)
invariants: No automated selection of decisions — maintainer must provide all answers
Recommended defaults from CLI instruction doc must be presented but not auto-accepted
postconditions: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/ID/DESIGN_DECISIONS.json')); assert all(f'DQ-{i}' in str(d) for i in [1,2,3,4])"
failure_modes: If maintainer does not respond: halt execution; write escalation report

STEP-B0-002-DECISIONS-DQ5-DQ7.purpose: Record maintainer decisions for DQ-5 (date-prefixed onboarding scheme P_ vs DOC-), DQ-6 (shim handling keep/deprecate/eliminate), DQ-7 (rollback granularity per-category vs per-file)
invariants: Recommended defaults: DQ-5 Option B, DQ-6 Option B, DQ-7 per-category
Final selection is maintainer's, not the recommended default
postconditions: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/ID/DESIGN_DECISIONS.json')); assert all(f'DQ-{i}' in str(d) for i in range(1,8))"
failure_modes: If maintainer does not respond: halt execution; write escalation report

STEP-B0-003-CREATE-EVIDENCE-INFRA.purpose: Create all phase-level evidence subdirectories under .state/evidence/authority_remediation/ and .state/evidence/remediation/ so downstream steps can write without path creation failures
invariants: Only directory creation — no file writes
Existing directories not overwritten
failure_modes: PREFLIGHT_FAIL if permission denied

STEP-B0-004-FREEZE-CONTRACT.purpose: Write a contract_frozen timestamp and schema_version to DESIGN_DECISIONS.json, confirming all 7 decisions are recorded and the plan may proceed to Track A and Track B execution phases
invariants: contract_frozen timestamp must be set to actual current time
Downstream phases must check contract_frozen before running
preconditions: python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/ID/DESIGN_DECISIONS.json')); assert all(f'DQ-{i}' in str(d) for i in range(1,8))"
failure_modes: If DQ entries missing: abort and re-trigger STEP-B0-001 or STEP-B0-002

STEP-B2-001-CLASSIFY-RUNTIME-ROLES.purpose: Classify authority for roles: allocator_entrypoint, allocator_backend, reservation_ledger, locking_utility, filesystem_watcher, gate_runner — read candidate files, assign canonical/provisional/contested status
invariants: No source files modified
Status must be one of: canonical, provisional, contested, missing — not found
failure_modes: AUTHORITY_CONFLICT if two files both qualify as canonical for same role

STEP-B2-002-CLASSIFY-WRITER-GATE-ROLES.purpose: Classify authority for roles: registry_writer, patch_generator, patch_applier, reconciler, promotion_bridge — read and compare candidates from REGISTRY_AUTOMATION and src_modules
invariants: No source files modified
If no candidate passes execution verification, role is marked contested or missing — not canonical
failure_modes: AUTHORITY_CONFLICT if two files both claim canonical writer status

STEP-B2-003-CLASSIFY-RESOLVER-ORCHESTRAT.purpose: Classify authority for roles: dir_id_handler, dir_identity_resolver, file_identity_resolver, healthcheck_entrypoint, ingest_orchestrator, column_runtime_engine, lookup_join_resolver
invariants: No source files modified
ingest_orchestrator status must account for multi-orchestrator competition documented in G1
failure_modes: AUTHORITY_CONFLICT for ingest_orchestrator: expected — record all three competitors and mark contested

STEP-B2-004-EMIT-AUTHORITY-MAP.purpose: Merge all three role-draft files into final CANONICAL_AUTHORITY_MAP.json; validate exactly 18 roles present; validate no role has two canonical entries
invariants: Exactly 18 role entries
No two entries share canonical status for same role
final_assessment block included using hardened schema from solution doc section 5.4
failure_modes: AUTHORITY_CONFLICT if merge reveals duplicate canonical — escalate before writing

STEP-B3-001-VERIFY-RUNTIME-IMPORTS.purpose: Run python -c import checks on all canonical/provisional files for roles: allocator_entrypoint, allocator_backend, filesystem_watcher, gate_runner, locking_utility
invariants: Read-only — no source files modified
imports_resolve set to yes/no/unknown based on actual check outcome, not assumption
failure_modes: IMPORT_BROKEN: record in output; do not abort step

STEP-B3-002-VERIFY-WRITER-GATE-IMPORTS.purpose: Run import checks on canonical/provisional files for roles: registry_writer, patch_generator, patch_applier, reconciler, promotion_bridge
invariants: Read-only verification
healthcheck_entrypoint import against P_01999000042260125106 (reconciler) not 125108
failure_modes: IMPORT_BROKEN: record broken imports; continue to next role

STEP-B3-003-VERIFY-ORCHESTRATOR-USAGE.purpose: Verify used_by_dominant_orchestrator for ingest_orchestrator candidates by searching import references in idpkg_runtime.py and pipeline_runner.py
invariants: used_by_dominant_orchestrator must only be yes if an actual import or invocation is found — not inferred from naming
failure_modes: If import not found: record used_by_dominant_orchestrator=no; do not infer yes

STEP-B3-004-EMIT-VERIFICATION-REPORT.purpose: Merge runtime, writer/gate, and orchestrator check results into EXECUTION_VERIFICATION_REPORT.json; one entry per role with all 6 execution_verification fields populated
invariants: All 6 execution_verification fields present per entry: imports_resolve, runtime_exec_verified, used_by_dominant_orchestrator, test_reference_found, contract_aligned, duplicate_shadow_absent
Exactly 18 verification records
failure_modes: If fewer than 18 verifications: abort; identify which roles are missing

STEP-B4-001-FIX-WRITE-POLICY-MISMATCH.purpose: Resolve WRITE_POLICY_IDPKG.yaml filename mismatch: inspect idpkg_runtime.py loader, verify UNIFIED_SSOT_REGISTRY_WRITE_POLICY.yaml structure equivalence, create WRITE_POLICY_IDPKG.yaml alias or patch loader to use actual filename
outputs: 01260207201000001250_REGISTRY/ID/4_config/WRITE_POLICY_IDPKG.yaml OR patched loader — exactly one chosen approach
invariants: Backup of original file created before any modification
Syntax validation run on any modified Python file
Only one fix approach applied — rename, alias, or code patch
failure_modes: IMPORT_BROKEN if loader cannot be patched to valid syntax
If neither rename nor code patch is safe: mark blocker as needs_manual_review

STEP-B4-002-FIX-HEALTHCHECK-IMPORT.purpose: Fix broken import in healthcheck.py that references P_01999000042260125108 instead of P_01999000042260125106 (registry_filesystem_reconciler); verify API compatibility before patching
inputs: healthcheck.py file (locate via registry or glob in ID/ dir)
invariants: Backup of original healthcheck.py created before modification
Syntax validation passes on patched file
API compatibility assessed before patching — api_compatibility field recorded

invariants: All 8 test cases must be present
Each test case must have: test_id, description, assertion, expected_result, enforcement_target

decision_ledger.context: Four files carry two 20-digit IDs concatenated in their name. get_file_id_from_name() extracts only the first ID. file_renamer.py body says second ID (01999000042260125010). Other three files have no body file_id.
options: A: Keep the first ID (what parser already extracts) — no parser changes needed
B: Keep the second ID — consistent with file_renamer.py body but unconfirmed for others
C: Decide per-file based on which ID has a registry entry; keep first as fallback if neither matches
decision_ledger.rationale: Most correct approach. Registry lookup before renaming catches the file_renamer.py mismatch definitively. First-ID fallback preserves backward compatibility for the three files without body file_id metadata. Avoids silent data loss.

decision_ledger.context: Once one ID is chosen as authoritative in a dual-ID filename, the dropped ID may still be referenced in imports, registry entries, or cross-references.
options: A: Create a cross-reference ALIAS registry entry mapping dropped_id to surviving_id
B: Grep-and-replace all references to the dropped ID across the codebase
C: Both A and B — cross-reference for safety plus grep-and-replace so alias is never needed at runtime
decision_ledger.rationale: Defense in depth. ALIAS entry provides traceability and catch-all for any reference missed by grep. Grep-and-replace eliminates runtime dependency on the alias. Together they leave no broken references.

decision_ledger.context: Both pre_commit_dir_id_check.py and registry_filesystem_reconciler.py carry file_id 01999000042260125106. One must be reallocated.
options: A: Reconciler keeps the ID; pre-commit hook gets a new one — reconciler is more complex and deeply integrated
B: Pre-commit hook keeps the ID; reconciler gets a new one — hook may be referenced in git config
decision_ledger.rationale: Reconciler is more deeply integrated and is referenced as the canonical authority in the authority map. Reallocating the pre-commit hook is lower risk. Verify no .pre-commit-config.yaml or git hook scripts reference the old hook filename before renaming.

decision_ledger.context: DOC-SCRIPT-0989, 0992, and 0993 are each assigned to two files. Manually-written scripts were assigned first; generator-emitted validators collided afterward due to a generator counter bug.
options: Keep the number on manually-written files; reallocate for generator-emitted validators; fix generator collision check
Keep the number on generator-emitted files; reallocate for manually-written files
decision_ledger.selected_option: Keep the number on manually-written files. Reallocate for the generator-emitted validators. Fix the generator counter logic.
decision_ledger.rationale: Manually-written files were assigned first per problem statement. Generator has a known counter bug that caused the collision. Fixing the generator prevents recurrence.

decision_ledger.context: Date-prefixed files have no embedded file_id or doc_id. When onboarding, should they receive P_ IDs or DOC- IDs? Validators matching DOC-SCRIPT patterns use shared.registry_config and argparse. Core pipeline files use core.registry_store.
options: A: All get P_ IDs — uniform, simple
B: Match convention of similar files — validators get DOC- IDs, core pipeline files get P_ IDs
C: All get DOC- IDs
decision_ledger.rationale: Role-based classification preserves the intent of the two naming schemes. DOC- IDs for validator scripts align with existing DOC-SCRIPT patterns. P_ IDs for core pipeline scripts align with existing P_ patterns. Mixed assignment is defensible from the analysis JSON role classifications.

decision_ledger.context: Three ungoverned files are compatibility shims using spec_from_file_location imports. Options range from keep permanently to eliminate immediately.
options: A: Onboard shims with their own IDs, keep them permanently
B: Onboard shims now with DEPRECATED status, schedule elimination by updating consumers
C: Eliminate shims immediately — find all importers, rewrite them, delete shims
decision_ledger.rationale: Immediate elimination (C) risks breaking consumers if any importer is missed. Onboarding with DEPRECATED status (B) governs the shims immediately while giving time to find and update all consumers safely. Permanent retention (A) leaves technical debt unaddressed.

decision_ledger.context: Should rollback be per-category (one rollback artifact per remediation phase) or per-file (one rollback record per individual rename/reallocation)?
options: Per-category: each phase produces a single rollback_manifest.json that reverses the entire phase atomically
Per-file: one rollback record per individual rename/reallocation
decision_ledger.rationale: Per-file granularity adds complexity without clear benefit since partial rollbacks within a category would leave the system in an inconsistent state. Per-category rollback is atomic and simpler. Rollback does not cascade — each phase's manifest is independent.
